value.strategy: The Sub-Fund is positioned to withstand a broad range of different environments. We remain conservatively positioned, with a relatively low weighting to equities and significant exposure to more defensive assets in the form of gold, short-dated inflation-linked bonds, and liquidity. We are also poised to act as and when opportunities arise, and will become more aggressive in increasing equity exposure if the current dislocation intensifies.
value.philosophy: Our approach is always to remain patient and lean into risk when we are rewarded to do so. The Strategy has greatly benefited from opportunistically buying from those indiscriminately selling in the past and should continue to do so in future, as we look to fulfil our dual mandate of preserving our investors’ capital and growing it in real terms over the long term.
value.selection_process: Within the equity component of the portfolio, we added to existing holdings and established new holdings. The majority of these purchases were made when equity markets had fallen in April. We have initiated several new investments and will become more aggressive in increasing equity exposure if the current dislocation intensifies. The Strategy has greatly benefited from opportunistically buying from those indiscriminately selling in the past.
value.asset_types: The fund invests in equities (e.g., Agilent Technologies, Alphabet, Chubb, Diageo, Adobe, L’Oréal, Hubbell, Canadian National Railway, LSEG, LVMH), and defensive assets such as gold, short-dated inflation-linked bonds, liquidity, and short-maturity Japanese Government Bonds.
value.real_objectives: The sub-fund aims to achieve growth in capital, ahead of inflation (UK Retail Prices Index), over the longer term (5 to 7 years). We look to fulfil our dual mandate of preserving our investors’ capital and growing it in real terms over the long term.
value.fund_history: The sub-fund was launched on 13 February 2012. The Strategy has greatly benefited from opportunistically buying from those indiscriminately selling in the past.
